title: Segregation of duties — toxic role combinations in IAM
exclusions: Does NOT assess business-justified exceptions; captured in a lookup.
evidence: Daily 'sod_toxic_combos' writes toxic-combo findings to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOX SoD — matching toxic combinations is the canonical SOX-ITGC test.
PCI 7.2 privilege assignment — SoD is a typical implementation.
A.5.3 segregation of duties — explicit policy control.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When any principal (human or service account) holds two roles listed as a toxic combo in sod_matrix.csv, the UC fires with principal, role_a, role_b.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Principals with role-combos that are expressly marked permitted_with_compensating=true do NOT fire.

spl:
| rest /services/iam/principals splunk_server=* | fields principal roles
| mvexpand roles
| join principal [| rest /services/iam/principals splunk_server=* | fields principal roles | mvexpand roles | rename roles as other_role]
| where roles!=other_role
| lookup sod_matrix.csv role_a=roles role_b=other_role OUTPUT toxic permitted_with_compensating
| where toxic="true" AND permitted_with_compensating!="true"
| table principal roles other_role
description: Enforces the SoD matrix every day across all principals. Toxic combos become visible the moment they are assigned, not at the next audit.
value: SoD failures are the top SOX ITGC finding in the Fortune-1000 cohort; automating them is the only defensible posture.
implementation: (1) Maintain sod_matrix.csv with the company's SoD rules; (2) ingest IAM state via REST; (3) run daily; (4) on finding, auto-open a remediation ticket.
visualization: Table of violations, network graph of role interactions, single-value 'principals with SoD violations'.
knownFalsePositives: Break-glass admin accounts require explicit permitted_with_compensating=true.
